provenance: Derived from a Cowork-collected sport/active venue lead sheet for seven non-Canggu districts (OtherBali_OTHER_DISTRICTS_sport_active_clubs_20260720.csv): Seminyak, Ubud, Nusa Dua, Uluwatu, Sanur, Jimbaran, Kuta. Canggu is intentionally excluded from this artifact — that district's sport-club leads were staged separately (data/data-ops/canggu-sport-leads/) and are being handled in a different session. Staged here as leads only — no editorial content has been written, no address has been verified, and no dedup against the live catalogue has been performed.
piiPolicy: Phone numbers and emails from the source sheet are omitted from this artifact (AGENTS.md guardrails #12/#14). They remain only in the original Cowork working sheet, which is not committed to this repository.
googleReviewDataPolicy: google_rating/google_reviews from the source sheet must never be published or shown anywhere public in Other Bali (guardrail #2 — no Google review scraping/republishing). Kept per-row under internalOnly for operator triage only; internalOnly.doNotPublish is true on every row as an explicit machine-checkable marker.
dedupeAgainstCatalogue: NOT performed. The authoring session has no live access to the production venues table (network-isolated sandbox). An operator with DB access must check each candidate against the current catalogue before import — many source rows are hotel/resort fitness or yoga facilities (e.g. Four Seasons, St. Regis, Grand Hyatt) that may already exist in the catalogue as venues under other categories; this was not cross-checked here.
categoryMapping: lib/types.ts VenueCategory has no dedicated sport-club category. Every row maps onto the existing 'fitness' or 'yoga' category (surf schools/shops map to the existing 'surf' category); the specific sport (padel, climbing, MMA, pilates, badminton...) is preserved in vibeTags/sportGroup so nothing is lost. No new category or entity was added.
districtMapping: Source district labels mapped onto existing DB district slugs (lib/districts.ts): Seminyak->seminyak, Ubud->ubud, 'Nusa Dua'->nusa-dua, Uluwatu->uluwatu-bukit, Sanur->sanur, Jimbaran->jimbaran, Kuta->kuta-legian. Rows whose area string is flagged 'edge' by Cowork (e.g. Sunset Road, Seminyak/Legian boundary) keep districtNeedsConfirmation=true — an operator should confirm the district before import rather than trusting the source label.
notes: These are venue CANDIDATES, not published venues. No editorial content (why_its_here/best_for/what_to_order) has been drafted for any row — that requires an actual site visit or a verified official-source read per AGENTS.md guardrail #10, not just a lead sheet.
readyToPublish is false for every row in this batch — it means 'not yet', not 'rejected'.

A large share of rows are hotel/resort in-house fitness centres and yoga studios (Four Seasons, Ritz-Carlton/Mandapa, St. Regis, Grand Hyatt, Conrad, Sofitel, Westin, Apurva Kempinski, etc.) — these need an explicit public-access check (day pass / outside-guest policy) before publishing as a standalone venue, since many hotel facilities are guest-only.
'leadSource'/'cowworkStatus'/'cowworkConfidence' describe Cowork's own sourcing method and confidence, not Other Bali verification.
gmapsUrl is a generated Google Maps SEARCH query built from name+area+district — not a verified pin. No street address was collected in the source sheet.
Canggu is excluded on purpose — see provenance field.
